components.value: V, S, M (diamond dust worth at least 1,000 gp) required to cast this spell, you can gain further bonuses on the d% roll made to determine the itemâs power. Every additional 100 gp in diamond dust you use in excess grants a cumulative +1 bonus to the roll, up to a maximum additional bonus equal to your caster level . If the item gains the ability to cast a spell, determine the spell it can cast randomly from spells you know of the appropriate level. If the item gains ranks in a skill, it gains ranks in a random skill in which you have at least 1 rank. If you roll above 100, then you can choose one item power on Table: Intelligent Item Powers to grant the item, and the item gains a special purpose. Roll once on Table: Intelligent Item Purpose and Table: Special Purpose Item Dedicated Powers to determine what the itemâs special purpose and dedicated powers are. These d% rolls are not modified by your caster level or any additional powdered gems you used to cast the spell. Once this spell ends, the item reverts to its previous natureâif you cast this spell on the item again, it gains entirely different powers as determined by a new set of rolls. description.value: This spell grants the target magic item temporary intelligence by siphoning off a tiny portion of your own mind to infuse the object. Only permanent magic items may be enhanced by this spellâone-use items and charged items cannot be affected by impart mind. Intelligent magic items cannot be affected by impart mind. When you cast impart mind on a magic item, the item gains an Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma score of 10 and gains your alignment. You have no special control over the item once it becomes intelligent, although since it has your alignment, personality conflicts with items you wield wonât be a problem. Determine the itemâs ego normally, as per the rules on intelligent items in the Core Rulebook. An item made intelligent via impart mind communicates via speech and has normal senses to a range of 60 feet. It speaks and reads one language known by youâif you know multiple languages, you may choose which language the item knows. When you cast impart mind on an item, roll once on Table: Intelligent Item Powers to randomly determine the itemâs powerâadd your caster level to this roll.
